description: Canonical (machine-readable) brain filing rules. The .md companion is the human explainer; this JSON is what `gbrain check-resolvable` audits against. Keep both in sync.

rules.description: A page whose primary subject is one person.
A page whose primary subject is one company or organization.
A page whose primary subject is a financing or M&A transaction.
A meeting transcript or minutes. Propagate entities to companies/ and people/ pages.
A reusable idea, framework, or mental model not tied to a specific person/company.
A multi-session piece of work with its own arc.

A discrete actionable item. Personal-canonical extension (added 2026-05-11). Required: status (open|in-progress|done|cancelled). Optional: project, due, assignee. Enables kanban-style views in NocoDB.

A point-in-time judgment record. Personal-canonical extension (added 2026-05-11). Required: decision (one-line statement), alternatives_considered. Optional: supersedes (prior slug). Distinct from concept and plan.

A prose deliverable tied to a parent project (and optionally a company). Personal-canonical extension (added 2026-05-11) -- not in upstream gbrain. Required frontmatter: project (slug). Optional: company (slug). Body should open with Related: [[project]], [[company]] so the wikilink graph reflects the relationship. Distinct from writing (essays) and guide (runbooks): a doc is artifact-scoped to a specific project arc.
A long-form analysis of a specific topic.
Public-sector, policy, or civic-issue content.
A piece of prose authored by the user.
A guide or runbook authored for future reference.
Technical references and tooling notes not tied to a specific company.
Financial reference data not tied to a single deal.
Personal-life content — kept separate from work.

Generative ideas the user might build, write, or expand later. Stub-shaped pages that mature over time. voice-note-ingest, archive-crawler, and similar capture-flavored skills file here when content is something to potentially act on.
Web-research output: what is NEW vs already-known about a topic, citation-checked claims, freshness deltas. perplexity-research and academic-verify file here.

Pages where the user is the primary author of the idea — original thinking, not summarizations of someone else's work. voice-note-ingest, archive-crawler, signal-detector route content here when the user is the originator.
Voice-note transcript holders, especially when the content is a random thought that doesn't cleanly fit originals/, concepts/, or another subject directory. voice-note-ingest is the primary writer.
Notes about the host OpenClaw agent itself, not the underlying entities.

Sanctioned exception to 'file by primary subject' for sui generis synthesized output that is one-of-one to a single book and a specific reader. Format-prefixed under media/<format>/ is allowed for synthesis output only, never for raw ingest. See _brain-filing-rules.md.

Same sanctioned exception as media/books/. One-of-one synthesis output of an article personalized for the reader. Distinct from raw article ingest, which goes to the article's primary-subject directory.

Date-keyed pages for events, calendar entries, or daily notes. Calendar imports land at daily/calendar/YYYY-MM-DD.md with attendees cross-linked to people/. Use when the primary subject is the date itself, not a person or topic.

Format-prefixed parent for media-by-source-format ingest. Subdirectories like media/x/{handle}/ hold X/Twitter archives, media/audio/ holds podcast/voice captures. The format-prefix lives only when the content is sui generis to the source format AND lacks a clean primary-subject directory. Prefer subject-by-subject filing; fall through to media/ only when the source format IS the unifying frame.

examples: working theories about a market or trend
rules.description: A hypothesis or working theory the user is testing over time. Use frontmatter `state: hypothesized|tested|confirmed|refuted|refined` to track lifecycle. Per Decision G (2026-05-04): passive convention only — no automated state machine. Synthesize phase may write here when the user expresses a thesis-shaped idea.

An observed pattern or shift in a market, technology, or behavior the user wants to track. Pages here are descriptive/observational. Per Decision G (2026-05-04): passive convention only. Synthesize phase may write here when content is best framed as a trend rather than a thesis or concept.

sources_dir.purpose: ONLY for raw data: bulk imports, API dumps, periodic captures. A page with a clear primary subject (person, company, concept) does NOT belong here.

notes: The PRIMARY SUBJECT of the content determines the directory, not the format or source skill.
When in doubt: what would you search for to find this page again?
Cross-link from related directories via back-links — do not duplicate content.

dream_synthesize_paths.description: Single source of truth for the v0.23 dream-cycle synthesize/patterns trusted-workspace allow-list. The cycle's synthesize phase reads this list and threads it as `allowed_slug_prefixes` to every subagent it dispatches; put_page enforces it server-side. Editing this list is the ONLY way to add a new directory the synthesis subagent may write to.
